heading: Unlading of passengers; penalty
label: 1454 Unlading of passengers; penalty
text: If any passenger is unladen from any vessel or vehicle without a special license or permit therefor issued by the appropriate customs officer, the master of such vessel or the person in charge of such vehicle and every other person who knowingly is concerned, or who aids therein, shall each be liable to a penalty of $1,000 for the first passenger and $500 for each additional such passenger so unladen.
notes: Amendments1986— substituted “$1,000 for the first passenger and $500 for each additional such passenger” for “$500 for each such passenger”.Pub. L. 99–5701970— substituted reference to appropriate customs officer for reference to collector.Pub. L. 91–271
Effective Date of 1970 AmendmentFor effective date of amendment by , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 91–271section 203 of Pub. L. 91–271section 1500 of this title
